pr.title: Match 8 functions across stage, fighter, and menu TUs
pr.author: davidfeira

summary: Merged decomp-matching batch across stage, fighter, and gm/menu code. The author summary describes 8 fully matched functions plus a gm_1601.h signature refinement; the decomp.dev bot reported +2604 matched code bytes, +48 matched data, 12 new matched items, and 9 improved unmatched items. The most reusable work was exact stage callback/data layout, typed gm history-tracker arguments, and fighter codegen tactics such as PAD_STACK, explicit user_data reloads, exact callback assignment, and replacing literals with matching data symbols where needed.

key_files.role: Brinstar stage data/callback reconstruction supporting grZebes_801D8558 match
Big Blue Route stage callback table and descriptor data; gobj setup match and random-route selector improvement
Pura/Poké Floats teardown driver match and helper signature correction
Ice Climbers/Popo special-state fixes and several small 100% matches reported by bot
Fighter dynamics work: Kirby-as-Jigglypuff hat dynamics installer matched; ftCo_8009CB40 improved but not exact per bot
Tiny knockback hook match by correcting ftCo_CalcYScaledKnockback argument order
Kirby copy-ability Special-N air-end driver match
Header refinement for typed gm_8016A9E8 signature

what_changed.details: Materialized Brinstar stage data including grZe_803E1A10, populated grZe_callbacks rows, and grZe_803E1B2C descriptor. Author says this supports grZebes_801D8558 matching as the per-stage gobj setup that installs display/proc callbacks and reports missing gobjs.

Replaced an extern StageCallbacks declaration with concrete grBb_Route_803E5E78[38], added route dat filename, descriptor struct, fmt string, and filename symbol. grBigBlueRoute_8020B9D4 now reports through descriptor-backed data; bot reported it reached 100%. The diff also adds grBigBlueRoute_8020C530, but bot classified it as an unmatched improvement at 98.72%, not exact.

Changed grPura_80213030 from void(void) to void(Ground_GObj*), passed arg0 from grPura_80211FD8, and matched the teardown sequence grPura_80212EF4 -> Ground_801C2FE0 -> grPura_80213030 -> mpLib_80055E24(0x18) -> lb_800115F4.

Adjusted checkNanaInRange to use a Vec3* to Nana cur_pos and compare ftNn_Init_8012300C(nana_gobj) == 1. Added PAD_STACK(32) to SpecialHiStart anim functions. Air startup now calls ftPp_SpecialHi_801220D4 and ftPp_SpecialHi_80121DD8 for the air-path fallback/advance. ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll was expanded to inline the motion-state change and reset death/take-damage callbacks; bot reported it as a 100% match.

ftCo_8009DB50 now indexes hat->hat_dynamics[4] through explicit bone_id locals, marks parts[bone_id].flags_b0, copies dynamic descriptors with lb_8000FD48, assigns FtPart_TopN, and finalizes with lb_80011710. __assert now uses __FILE__. The diff also adds ftCo_8009CB40, but bot listed it as 92.02% rather than matched.

ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90 selects the air-end motion state by cmd_vars[0] and hat.kind == FTKIND_MARS, reloads fields via gobj->user_data casts, calls ftCommon_8007D5D4, passes ftKb_Init_804D9574 and ftKb_Init_804D9570 into Fighter_ChangeMotionState, and installs efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll around hitlag when x2219_b0 is true.
ftCo_800D0EC8 now returns -ftCo_CalcYScaledKnockback(p_ftCommonData->x310, fp->x34_scale.y, Fighter_804D6524->x30), fixing the argument order needed for the 100% match.

Implemented gm_8016A9E8 as a 27-slot MRU push for lbl_8046B668: find first arr2 sentinel -2, shift arr1/arr2 entries down, write arg1 and arg0 at slot 0, return found+1 or -1. Header changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to int gm_8016A9E8(u8 arg0, s8 arg1).

smart_moves: Used concrete stage callback tables and descriptor structs instead of leaving data as extern/placeholder, which improved both code and .data matching for grzebes and grbigblueroute.
Preserved exact compiler behavior in fighter functions with PAD_STACK, explicit gobj->user_data casts, scoped temporaries, and helper inlining rather than only using cleaner GET_FIGHTER-style source.

Matched constant/data references deliberately: ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90 uses extern f32 symbols ftKb_Init_804D9574/ftKb_Init_804D9570, grbigblueroute routes OSReport through staged fmt/file symbols, and ftdynamics uses __FILE__ in the assert.
Tightened function signatures where the body proved the types, especially gm_8016A9E8(u8, s8) and grPura_80213030(Ground_GObj*), avoiding UNK_PARAMS and call-site mismatches.
Kept the PR non-regressing by dropping an earlier gricemt const-section reorder after CI showed a 4-byte regression in neighboring grIceMt_801F7A2C.
decomp_lessons: Stage setup matches often require reconstructing adjacent StageCallbacks rows, stage descriptor data, datfile strings, and report-format strings; code-only edits may leave nearby .data and setup functions unmatched.

For small fighter functions, source cleanliness can be secondary to load ordering: explicit user_data reloads, boolean comparisons such as == 1, and temporary pointer choices can be the difference between 99.x% and 100%.
Header typing should follow matched bodies. The gm_8016A9E8 implementation shows arg0 as u8, arg1 as s8, and int return; carrying UNK_PARAMS would hide useful ABI evidence.
Do not reorder const/data to force a local match if it regresses a neighbor. The removed gricemt attempt is a useful warning to isolate risky data-section work in follow-up PRs.
Automated decomp.dev output may count data sections and extra small functions differently from the PR title/body. Record both the author's claimed matches and the bot's match/improvement tables.
naming_conventions: Stage file prefixes remain stage-specific: grZe_* for Zebes/Brinstar data, grBb_Route_* for Big Blue Route data, grPu_* for Pura/Poké Floats callbacks.
Stage callback arrays use StageCallbacks entries with callback0/callback1/callback2/callback3-style function pointer rows and flag words such as 0xC0000000, 0x80000000, and 0x40000000.
Address-suffixed decomp symbols are retained for unknown functions/data: grZe_803E1B2C, grBb_Route_803E617C, gm_8016A9E8, ftCo_8009DB50.
gm_1601.h keeps address comments beside prototypes, e.g. /* 16A9E8 */ int gm_8016A9E8(u8 arg0, s8 arg1).
Fighter motion/callback fields are named through existing project conventions: pre_hitlag_cb, post_hitlag_cb, death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, accessory4_cb, cmd_vars, cur_anim_frame.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PAD_STACK was used in ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90, ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Anim, and ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll to preserve stack shape.
Explicit casts like ((Fighter*) gobj->user_data)->cmd_vars[0] were used instead of relying only on a cached Fighter* to match reload/register behavior.
Replacing 1.0F and 0.0F arguments with ftKb_Init_804D9574 and ftKb_Init_804D9570 matched the original data loads in Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
The ftdynamics match used a separate s32 bone_id local and repeated hat->hat_dynamics[4]->ftDynamicBones->array[i] access rather than a BoneDynamicsDesc* local, aligning codegen for ftCo_8009DB50.
For checkNanaInRange, using a Vec3* to cur_pos and an explicit ftNn_Init_8012300C(...) == 1 comparison affected generated code.
ftCo_800D0EC8 shows that even a one-line arithmetic hook can hinge on exact callee argument order.

grBigBlueRoute_8020C530 used byte-stride access to xC8 entries, bit-7 availability tests, HSD_Randi(count), and an assert fallback; bot still reported 98.72%, so this tactic was an improvement but not a final match.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code 66.28% (+0.07%, +2604 bytes) and matched data 38.70% (+0.00%, +48 bytes).

Bot listed 12 new matched items, including gm_8016A9E8, grBigBlueRoute_8020B9D4, ftCo_8009DB50, ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll, grZebes_801D8558, ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Anim, grPura_80211FD8, .sdata/.sdata2 items, ftCo_800D0EC8, and ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90.
Bot also listed 9 improved unmatched items, notably ftCo_8009CB40 at 92.02%, grbigblueroute .data at 95.57%, grBigBlueRoute_8020C530 at 98.72%, and grzebes .data at 79.59%.

Author notes say an earlier gricemt commit with fn_801F9338, fn_801F9448, fn_801F9558 and a const-section reorder was dropped after CI found a 4-byte regression in grIceMt_801F7A2C; those matches were deferred to a follow-up.
searchable_terms: Match 8 functions across stage fighter menu TUs
follow_up_queries: Find the follow-up PR for gricemt fn_801F9338, fn_801F9448, fn_801F9558 after the grIceMt_801F7A2C 4-byte regression was avoided.
Revisit ftCo_8009CB40, which this PR improved to 92.02% but did not fully match according to the bot.
Revisit grBigBlueRoute_8020C530, which this PR improved to 98.72% but did not fully match according to the bot.
Look for later cleanup that moves local extern f32 ftKb_Init_804D9570/ftKb_Init_804D9574 into a shared Kirby header if project style allows.
Search other stage TUs for the same StageCallbacks + stage descriptor reconstruction pattern used by grZe_803E1B2C and grBb_Route_803E617C.
